dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7241 IH: Protect Veterans from the Theft of Health and Identifying Information in Electronic Forms Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Budzinski (for herself and Mr. Barrett) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Veterans' Affairs

form.official-title: To amend title 38, United States Code, to prohibit the Secretary of Veterans Affairs from entering into a contract pursuant to which the contractor may sell sensitive personal information maintained by the Secretary and to ensure the protection of personal information in certain contracts of the Department.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Protect Veterans from the Theft of Health and Identifying Information in Electronic Forms Act or the Protect Veterans from the THIEF Act.

section.header: Prohibition of the sale of sensitive personal information maintained by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs
section.text: Section 5725 of title 38, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following new subsection:

subsection.header: Prohibition of sale of sensitive personal information
subsection.text: The Secretary may not enter into a contract that permits the contractor to sell (or otherwise disclose for consideration) sensitive personal information to another entity.

section.header: Protection of personal information in contracts of the Department of Veterans Affairs

subsection.text: Not later than one year after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Veterans Affairs shall—

paragraph.text: ensure that each covered contract includes, or is modified to include, a clause prohibiting covered information from being monetized, sold, or otherwise misused by any contractor, including any subcontractor or affiliate thereof, or other non-Department of Veterans Affairs entity; and

issue a directive or other policy providing guidance to employees and contractors of the Department on how to identify the monetization, sale, or misuse of covered information in order to ensure contractors are in compliance with clauses in covered contracts included pursuant to paragraph (1).

subsection.text: Not later than one year after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees a report that includes—

paragraph.text: a copy of the contract clause required by subsection (a)(1);
the guidance required by subsection (a)(2); and
a summary of any other actions taken to comply with subsection (a).
The term appropriate congressional committees means the Committees on Veterans’ Affairs of the House of Representatives and the Senate.
The term covered contract means a contract of the Department of Veterans Affairs that provides for the handling of covered information and is entered into—

subparagraph.text: after the date of the enactment of this Act; or
before the date of the enactment of this Act and does not expire before the date of the enactment of this Act.
means protected health information or personally identifiable information, including such information that has been anonymized; and

clause.text: section 552a of title 5, United States Code;
section 5701 or 7332 of title 38 United States Code;
parts 160, 161, and 164 of title 45, Code of Federal Regulations; and
any other provision of law, as determined by the Secretary.
